transcript.text:
I’ve finished the runtime trace. The important real-world pattern is: consumers register a renderer with `echarts.use(...)`, then render one of the exported chart components with ECharts option props and sometimes child helpers like `Title`, `Tooltip`, `Legend`, `Dataset`, or `AxisBreak`; there are no app-level providers or global CSS files to copy over in this repo.

Plan from here:
1. Replace the starter Storybook examples with stories that exercise the real library exports and composition patterns from the README and source.
2. Move the shared runtime setup into `.storybook/preview.tsx`, primarily the default renderer registration and any layout defaults ECharts needs to render consistently.
3. Keep each story thin by rendering the exported chart components directly with realistic option objects, instead of building custom harnesses.
4. Run storybook-focused Vitest screenshots and `tsc --noEmit`, then tighten anything that still renders empty or types poorly.

I’ve finished the runtime trace. The important real-world pattern is: consumers register a renderer with `echarts.use(...)`, then render one of the exported chart components with ECharts option props and sometimes child helpers like `Title`, `Tooltip`, `Legend`, `Dataset`, or `AxisBreak`; there are no app-level providers or global CSS files to copy over in this repo.

1. Replace the starter Storybook examples with stories that exercise the real library exports and composition patterns from the README and source.
2. Move the shared runtime setup into `.storybook/preview.tsx`, primarily the default renderer registration and any layout defaults ECharts needs to render consistently.
3. Keep each story thin by rendering the exported chart components directly with realistic option objects, instead of building custom harnesses.
4. Run storybook-focused Vitest screenshots and `tsc --noEmit`, then tighten anything that still renders empty or types poorly.

content.text: The first browser pass exposed a real runtime gap in one story rather than the preview setup: the heatmap story was passing a `grid` option even though `HeatmapChart` doesn’t register `GridComponent` by default. I’m trimming that story back to the actual registered runtime so the story stays thin and the render path matches the exported component behavior.

I found the second part of that heatmap failure: unlike the line, bar, and scatter wrappers, `HeatmapChart` doesn’t bundle cartesian axes for you. I’m keeping the story thin by using the component’s existing `use` prop to register `GridComponent` in the same render path, then I’ll rerun the heatmap and the full story set.

**What Changed**
- Storybook now matches the real library runtime instead of the starter demo. [preview.tsx](/Users/kasperpeulen/code/storybook/storybook-eval/trials/2026-04-03T09-50-54Z-7f33de2e/project/.storybook/preview.tsx#L1) registers `CanvasRenderer` once and provides a stable full-width chart shell; [main.ts](/Users/kasperpeulen/code/storybook/storybook-eval/trials/2026-04-03T09-50-54Z-7f33de2e/project/.storybook/main.ts#L1) only loads real `src/**/*.stories.tsx` stories plus the eval docs.
- I replaced the starter Button/Header/Page stories with thin chart stories based on the actual exports and usage patterns in this repo: [LineChart.stories.tsx](/Users/kasperpeulen/code/storybook/storybook-eval/trials/2026-04-03T09-50-54Z-7f33de2e/project/src/LineChart.stories.tsx#L1), [BarChart.stories.tsx](/Users/kasperpeulen/code/storybook/storybook-eval/trials/2026-04-03T09-50-54Z-7f33de2e/project/src/BarChart.stories.tsx#L1), [PieChart.stories.tsx](/Users/kasperpeulen/code/storybook/storybook-eval/trials/2026-04-03T09-50-54Z-7f33de2e/project/src/PieChart.stories.tsx#L1), [ScatterChart.stories.tsx](/Users/kasperpeulen/code/storybook/storybook-eval/trials/2026-04-03T09-50-54Z-7f33de2e/project/src/ScatterChart.stories.tsx#L1), [GraphChart.stories.tsx](/Users/kasperpeulen/code/storybook/storybook-eval/trials/2026-04-03T09-50-54Z-7f33de2e/project/src/GraphChart.stories.tsx#L1), and [HeatmapChart.stories.tsx](/Users/kasperpeulen/code/storybook/storybook-eval/trials/2026-04-03T09-50-54Z-7f33de2e/project/src/HeatmapChart.stories.tsx#L1).
- I did not add `preview-body.html` because the real codebase has no portals, portal roots, fetch mocking needs, provider tree, or browser-state setup to copy.
